commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=480ad24da15b0d7575f053e4384a06ad567f4e65)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes fallback-to-empty catches from two memory-wiki existing-page rereads and adds an ingest regression test for read-error propagation.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +54. Total +62 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has two existing-page read sites that convert read errors to empty strings before note preservation, and the linked issue provides a concrete fault-injection path; I did not run tests in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor proof and maintainer canonical-branch selection; automation cannot supply the contributor's real setup proof.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): propagate read errors instead of silently wiping human notes
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

memory-wiki silently discards the user `## Notes` human block when the re-read of the existing wiki page transiently fails during source re-ingest. `.catch(() => "")` turns a real read error into `existing = ""`, which routes past `preserveHumanNotesBlock` into the empty-template write.

Closes #98345

## Fix

Remove `.catch(() => "")` from two locations:
- `ingest.ts:90` — source ingest re-read  
- `source-page-shared.ts:55` — imported source page re-read

A transient read failure (EIO, path-mismatch concurrent-write race) now propagates instead of being silently treated as "no existing page". The caller already retries the same transient race via `writeGuardedVaultPage`, so a failed read succeeds on retry while keeping human notes intact.

**2 files, 12 lines (+ tests)**

## Evidence

### Real behavior proof — injected EIO read failure

```
npx vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest.test.ts --no-watch
  Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  2 passed (2)

npx vitest run extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-page-shared.test.ts --no-watch
  Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  3 passed (3)
```

The proof test in `ingest.test.ts`:
1. Creates a vault and ingests a source page
2. Adds user `## Notes` content via human-notes markers
3. Updates the source to trigger re-ingest
4. Injects a one-shot EIO failure on the existing-page re-read
5. Verifies the error PROPAGATES (does not silently wipe notes)
6. Verifies notes survive after the failed ingest

### Before/after

| | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| EIO on re-read | `.catch(() => "")` → empty → notes wiped | error thrown → caller retries |
| Notes survive | ❌ | ✅ |

### Formatting & lint

```
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/memory-wiki/src/ingest.ts ...
  All matched files use the correct format.
git diff --check  (clean)
pnpm exec oxlint ...  (clean)
```

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] memory-wiki extension

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No
